content: Emir of Qatar Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani and Jordan’s King Abdullah II spoke on the phone today to discuss regional developments and “their implications for peace and stability”, according to a statement from Qatar. “Both sides stressed the need for an immediate halt to the escalation and for opening channels of dialogue to preserve the region’s security and prevent further tensions,” the statement said. “The two leaders also condemned the targeting of Qatar, Jordan, and other Arab countries with Iranian ballistic missiles, affirming continued coordination between the two countries to take all necessary measures to safeguard sovereignty and enhance security and stability.”

title: Hezbollah condemns US-Israel attack
content: Lebanon’s Hezbollah has condemned the US-Israeli attack on Iran, calling it a “continuation of the arrogant and domineering approach” by Israel and the US in the region. The Lebanese group expressed solidarity with Iran but failed to say whether it would take any action in support of its longtime ally.

title: Death toll from elementary school attack in Iran rises to 85: Report
content: The number of people killed in an Israeli strike on an elementary girls’ school in Minab, in the southern province of Hormozgan, has now risen to 85, according to the semi-official Tasnim news agency. The number could not be immediately verified.

title: CENTCOM says IRGC claims of US military casualties are ‘false’
content: In comments made to Al Jazeera, a spokesperson for the US military’s Central Command (CENTCOM) has said that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard’s (IRGC) claims of killing and wounding 200 US soldiers are false. It said that the IRGC’s claims of damaging a CENTCOM ship are also false.

title: E3 call for end to Iran’s nuclear programe, urge return to negotiating table
content: The E3 countries – France, Germany, the United Kingdom – who previously were in negotiations with Iran going back many years to try to bring about a peaceful end to its nuclear programme, have put out a joint statement. They called for Iran to end its nuclear programme, to curb its ballistic missile programme, to refrain from what it called its destabilising activities in the region, and to cease its repressive actions against its own people. They said they did not take part in these strikes, but they are in close cooperation and consultation with Israel, the United States and other regional partners. They are also making it clear that they believe the Iranian government should return to the negotiating table.

title: Qatar urges people in country to ‘remain indoors’ until situation stabilises
content: We have an update from Qatar’s Interior Ministry, which has urged people in the country to “remain indoors” amid the Iranian attacks. “Stay away from windows and open areas, and avoid leaving their homes except in cases of absolute necessity until the situation stabilizes,” it said in a social media post. In light of the current developments and the Iranian targeting of the State’s territory, the Ministry of Interior urges citizens, residents and visitors to remain indoors, stay away from windows and open areas, and avoid leaving their homes except in cases of absolute necessity… — Ministry of Interior – Qatar (@MOI_QatarEn) February 28, 2026

title: Four injured in Dubai ‘incident’: Media office
content: Authorities in Dubai have confirmed that an incident occurred in a building in the Palm Jumeirah area that resulted in injuries. “Emergency response teams were deployed immediately and the site has been secured. Dubai Civil Defence has confirmed that the resulting fire is now under control,” the Dubai Media Office said in a statement. According to the media office, four people were injured and transferred to medical facilities for treatment. “Authorities continue to take all necessary measures to safeguard the public,” it said. Dubai authorities confirm that an incident occurred in a building in the Palm Jumeirah area. Emergency response teams were deployed immediately and the site has been secured. Dubai Civil Defence has confirmed that the resulting fire is now under control. Four individuals… — Dubai Media Office (@DXBMediaOffice) February 28, 2026

title: More loud blasts heard in Doha
content: At least three new explosions have just been heard in Qatar’s capital, Doha.

title: Photos: Projectiles over Dubai
content: The flare of a projectile is seen over the skyline of Dubai, United Arab Emirates [Fatima Shbair/AP Photo] The flare of a projectile is seen over the skyline of Dubai, United Arab Emirates [AFP] [AFP]

title: Iran calls on the UN to ‘address the US and Israeli regime’s acts of aggression’
content: Iran has submitted a letter to the UN Security Council and Secretary-General Antonio Guterres, in which it called on the UN to “bear responsibility for international peace”. The letter said, “Iran urgently calls upon the members of the Security Council to convene, without delay, an emergency meeting of the Council to address the US and Israeli regime’s acts of aggression, breach of peace which is a real and serious threat to international peace and security, and to take the necessary and immediate measures to halt this unlawful use of force and to ensure accountability.” “The Islamic Republic of Iran calls on all Member States of the United Nations that bear responsibility for international peace and security to unequivocally condemn this act of aggression and to undertake urgent and collective measures to confront it, as it undoubtedly poses an unprecedented threat to regional as well as global peace and security.”

title: UNSC to meet today at France’s request
content: France’s Foreign Minister Jean-Noel Barrot says on X that his country has requested a meeting of the UN Security Council at 21:00 GMT today. The purpose of the meeting, according to Barrot, is to “address the situation in Iran and the Middle East”.

title: Netanyahu, Trump speak on the phone
content: The office of Israel’s prime minister has published a picture showing Benjamin Netanyahu holding a phone call. The description said Netanyahu was having a conversation with US President Donald Trump. ראש הממשלה בנימין נתניהו בשיחתו עם נשיא ארה"ב דונלד טראמפ. pic.twitter.com/yZMMgBqGl8 — ראש ממשלת ישראל (@IsraeliPM_heb) February 28, 2026

title: PM Starmer says UK has strengthened ‘defensive capabilities in the region’
content: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has said the UK was not involved in the US-Israel military attacks against Iran, stressing that his country “played no role in these strikes”. Starmer said the UK stands in support and solidarity with “partners across the region” that were targeted. “As part of our commitments to the security of our allies in the Middle East we have a range of defensive capabilities in the region – which we’ve recently taken steps to strengthen,” he added. “Our forces are active and British planes are in the sky today as part of coordinated regional defensive operations to protect our people, our interests, and our allies – as Britain has done before, in line with international law. The prime minister also said the UK had “stepped up protections for British bases and personnel to their highest level”. “We are also reaching out to UK nationals in the region and doing everything we can to support them,” Starmer said.

title: Photos: Anti-US protest in Tehran
content: Iranian demonstrators protest against the US-Israeli strikes, in Tehran, Iran [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters] [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters] [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters] [Majid Asgaripour/West Asia News Agency/Reuters]

title: Germany issues travel warning for several Arab countries
content: The German Foreign Ministry has issued a travel warning for the following countries: Bahrain Iraq Israel Jordan Qatar Kuwait Oman Saudi Arabia United Arab Emirates.

title: Israeli army says it is striking ‘missile launchers’ in Iran
content: The Israeli army said its air force has begun new air strikes targeting “missile launchers” in Iran.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, occupied West Bank
title: Iranian missiles strike central Israel; no casualties reported
content: Initial reports suggest two areas suffered from direct impact of Iranian missiles in central Israel. Emergency crews have been called there but no casualties have been reported so far. This comes following more Iranian missiles directed towards Israeli territory – this has been nonstop since the morning hours. The Tel Aviv and Haifa area on the coast have been most affected by sirens and warnings.

title: Drone targets Kuwait International Airport
content: A drone has targeted Kuwait International Airport, resulting in “minor injuries” to a number of employees, in addition to “limited material damage” to the passenger building, according to The General Authority of Civil Aviation in Kuwait. Its official spokesperson, Abdullah al-Rajhi, stated that the authorities immediately initiated “approved emergency procedures, where the incident was handled, and the site was secured”.

title: Explosions have been heard in Doha since the morning
content: Throughout the morning, we’ve seen multiple waves of what has become a bit of a pattern, but it seems to be escalating. The initial rounds were about three or four explosions at a time, interceptions happening overhead. You can see plumes of smoke in the sky where those missiles were being targeted by Patriot and THAAD defence systems. But in the hours that followed, they went from three or four to about six or seven in the last 30 minutes. What we heard was a round of explosions, about seven or eight different explosions. I can say those sounded like sustained anti-aircraft fire, commonly used against unmanned drones. Now, Iran has many unmanned drones, but we don’t know what exactly is being used. The promise from the US is that this campaign is only the first day of what will go on for at least a week, if not longer. So what we are likely to see is more debris falling like what we have seen today. Smoke in Doha’s sky as Qatar intercepts Iranian missiles [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera]

title: UNSC to meet on Iran, Guterres warns of wider regional conflict
content: We are receiving reports that the UN Security Council is set to meet later today over the joint Israeli and US attacks on Iran and the current military escalation across the Middle East. UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has, meanwhile, issued a statement calling for an immediate cessation of hostilities and warning that the failure to do so risks “a wider regional conflict with grave consequences for civilians and regional stability.” Guterres also said all member atates must “respect their obligations under international law, including the Charter of the United Nations”, which prohibits “the threat of the use of force against the territorial integrity or political independence of any state”.

title: Moscow views attacks as ‘reckless’
content: Russia’s President Vladimir Putin has discussed the situation with Russia’s security council. There were no other lines coming from that, but there were quite a few coming from the Foreign Ministry. We know that Iran’s Foreign Minister called Russia’s foreign minister, Sergey Lavrov, and they discussed the situation. Lavrov said he would call to convene the UN Security Council. Now, a statement that came out from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, there were a few interesting lines from there. The ministry called the attack “reckless “and said there’s absolutely no doubt that it was unprovoked and premeditated, an attack on a foreign sovereign nation, which is in violation of international law. We’ve also heard from the Foreign Ministry that there are concerns about radiological risk in the region. This is in reference to the Bushehr nuclear power plant; we’ve received information that there was no damage done in strikes that hit the plant, but the Russian company operating it has evacuated around 100 people from Iran.

title: ‘Trump adopted Israeli red lines and attacked Iran’
content: President of the Quincy Institute for Responsible Statecraft, Trita Parsi, says there was no “international legal basis” for the US’s attack on Iran despite Trump’s speech justifying the operation. “In an ever-changing effort at justifying what clearly is, not only an illegal war, but also a clear betrayal of the promise [President] Donald Trump made to his voters when he said he would be the peace president and he would rally against these unnecessary and forever wars in the Middle East and beyond”, Parsi told Al Jazeera. Parsi explained that Trump “adopted” the Israeli red line and their reasoning, which had led him on a path towards an attack. “The Iranians fear surrender more than they fear war and were not in the mood and had no need to surrender to Trump, and as a result, now we have seen Trump was willing to escalate further”, he said.

title: Loud bangs heard in Dubai: Report
content: We’re getting reports of loud bangs being heard in Dubai, in the UAE. Reuters quoted a witness as saying that a plume of smoke and fire could be seen in the city’s Palm Island neighbourhood.

title: Editor’s Choice: What to read and watch right now
content: We’ve published a number of pieces in recent hours; here are a few highlights: US and Israel attack Iran: What we know so far Al Jazeera reporter on the streets of Tehran as missiles hit the city Multiple Arab states that host US assets targeted in Iran retaliation Who is Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei and why is he a possible target? Airspace closed, airlines halt flights as US, Israel attack, Iran responds A timeline of talks and threats leading up to attacks And there’s plenty more here .

title: Iran’s supreme leader to speak soon, Al-Alam TV says
content: Iran’s ⁠Supreme Leader ⁠Ayatollah Ali Khamenei ⁠will speak within minutes, ‌Iran’s Al-Alam TV reported. We’ll bring you more on that shortly.

title: A degree of disagreement among European leaders over US-Israeli attacks on Iran
content: From the EU perspective, we’ve now had a statement from the E3, which is three countries, Germany, France and the United Kingdom, from their respective heads of state, about what has been happening. It’s quite long; it says that they did not participate in these strikes but are in close contact with their international partners, including the United States, Israel, and partners in the region. Ultimately, it said, the Iranian people must be allowed to determine their future. Remember that these three countries have been part of those seeking to, over the course of the last 10-15 years, back to the Obama administration, trying to find peaceful ways to prevent Iran from getting a nuclear weapon. Contrast that with the view of the Spanish prime minister, condemning both Israel and the United States and Iran, and we see some degree of disagreement among European leaders.

title: NATO ‘closely following’ developments in Iran
content: NATO ⁠is closely ⁠following developments in Iran ‌and the region, NATO ⁠spokesperson Allison Hart ⁠says.

title: China urges ceasefire, says Iran’s sovereignty must be respected
content: China’s ⁠Ministry of Foreign Affairs has expressed deep ⁠concerns over the developments in the region, urging an immediate ceasefire. It said Iran’s national sovereignty, ⁠security, and territorial integrity ⁠must be respected and ⁠urged all ⁠sides to avoid ⁠further escalation and ‌resume dialogue.

title: Explosions heard in Iran’s Shiraz
content: The Fars news agency is reporting several explosions in Shiraz, in the southwestern part of Iran.

title: Iran’s foreign minister says Khamenei alive ‘as far as I know’: Report
content: In an interview with NBC News, Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has said that Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is alive “as far as I know”. Here are some of his other remarks: Trump’s message about regime change in Iran is “mission impossible” He said there is no communication with the US as of now, adding that is Washington wants to talk, they know how to reach out to the Iranians. But he added that attacks should stop before there is a possibility to talk. He said one or two commanders were killed, but most officials were alive. The diplomat stressed that there is a possibility of reaching a deal which can guarantee that Iran’s nuclear programme is peaceful. Iran does not have the capability to hit the US and will not build missiles that could do that

title: Republican Thomas Massie says he’s ‘opposed to this war’
content: Thomas Massie, a US Republican lawmaker, has said he is “opposed to this war”. “This is not ‘America First’,” he wrote on social media, saying he would work to force a Congressional vote on war with Iran. “The Constitution requires a vote, and your Representative needs to be on record as opposing or supporting this war,” he added. I am opposed to this War. This is not “America First.” When Congress reconvenes, I will work with @RepRoKhanna to force a Congressional vote on war with Iran. The Constitution requires a vote, and your Representative needs to be on record as opposing or supporting this war. — Thomas Massie (@RepThomasMassie) February 28, 2026

title: At least 70 killed in southern Iran province: Official tells Iranian news agency
content: The head of Hormozgan Medical Sciences says at least 70 people have been killed and more than 90 wounded in attacks on Minab and Jask, both in Hormozgan province in southern Iran, the semi-official Fars news agency is reporting. Earlier, we reported that a strike hit an elementary girls’ school in Minab, killing more than 50 people. The numbers could not be immediately verified.

title: ‘All I want is freedom for the people’: Trump
content: The US president has given a brief remark to the Washington Post, his first comment since his recorded video message earlier today. “I want a safe nation, and that’s what we’re going to have,” Trump claimed. “All I want is freedom for the people.” Iranian state media has reported that more than 50 people were killed in a strike that hit an elementary girls’ school in Minab, a city in the Hormozgan province of southern Iran. In the video posted on Truth Social overnight, Trump had encouraged the Iranian people to take over their government, saying it would “be probably your only chance for generations”.

title: Air raid alerts sound in Israel
content: The Israeli military says alerts have “been activated in several areas across the country following the detection of missiles launched from Iran toward Israeli territory”. In a post on X, it said, “at this hour, the air force is operating to intercept and strike wherever necessary to remove the threat.” In a separate statement, it said it had completed an extensive wave of strikes against Iran’s strategic defence systems. It claimed one of the targets attacked was an advanced SA-65 air defence system, located in the Kermanshah area in western Iran.

title: More updates from Tehran
content: One of the targeted sites in Tehran was Narmak to the east, around the believed residence of former populist President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad. No details were confirmed yet, but numerous videos are available online. According to the IRGC-affiliated Fars news agency, paramilitary Basij forces will begin patrolling the streets of the capital tonight.

title: Turkiye calls on all parties to cease attacks ‘immediately’, offers to mediate
content: The Turkish Foreign Ministry has issued a statement condemning “provocations” that could lead to further military escalation and called on all parties to cease attacks “immediately”. It also said it was ready to support mediation efforts.

title: Qatar and UAE leaders condemn Iranian missile strikes
content: Qatar’s Emir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani has discussed the situation in the region with the president of the United Arab Emirates, according to a statement from his office. Sheikh Tamim and the UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan condemned the targeting of Qatar, the UAE, and other Arab countries by Iranian missiles, the statement said. “Their Highnesses affirmed the solidarity of the two countries with all brotherly Arab countries that are subjected to aggression, and the continuation of joint coordination in a way that preserves the security of the region and the dignity of its peoples,” it added.

title: Who is Ali Shamkhani?
content: While former Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Commander Ali Shamkhani was among those Israel targeted in its attack on Iran this morning, it is not the first time he’s been targeted. Last year, during Israel and the US’s 12-day war, Shamkhani, 70, was pulled out alive from under rubble following an attack on his home in Tehran. But he first rose through the ranks of the IRGC during the Iran-Iraq war in 1980. By 1982, he was appointed as deputy to IRGC commander-in-chief Mohsen Rezaei, and by the war’s end in 1988, he was commanding the organisation’s ground forces and appointed as minister of the IRGC. The following year, with Ayatollah Ali Khamenei newly appointed, Shamkhani was transferred to the navy and, within a year, was given simultaneous control of both the regular and IRGC navies. From 1997 to 2005, Shamkhani was appointed as the defence minister under former President Mohammad Khatami. In 2015, Shamkhani was involved in implementing the nuclear agreement. After leaving the Supreme National Security Council in 2023, where he had been appointed since 2013, Shamkhani has served as a political adviser to Khamenei.

title: Saudi Arabia confirms Iran targeted Riyadh, eastern region
content: A statement by the Saudi Foreign Ministry has confirmed that Iran targeted Riyadh and the kingdom’s eastern region, adding that the attacks were repelled. “These attacks cannot be justified under any pretext or in any way, and they came despite the Iranian authorities knowing that the Kingdom had confirmed that it would not allow its airspace and territory to be used to target Iran,” it added.

title: Russia, US issue evacuation advice
content: The Russian embassy in Iran has urged Russian citizens to leave Iran via Armenia or Azerbaijan. The two countries border Iran in the northwest. Meanwhile, the US embassy in Lebanon has urged its citizens to leave the country in a post on X.

title: Germany, France and UK condemn Iranian attacks, want talks to resume
content: ⁠Germany, ⁠France and the United Kingdom condemned Iranian attacks ⁠on countries in the ⁠region in response to US and Israeli attacks. “We reiterate our commitment to regional stability and to the protection of civilian ⁠life,” French President ⁠Emmanuel Macron, German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and ⁠British Prime Minister ⁠Keir Starmer ⁠said in a joint statement, adding that they ‌wanted a resumption of negotiations.

title: Latest updates from Tehran
content: There’s heavy traffic outbound from Tehran, especially towards the provinces to the north. Authorities are facilitating the movement, including by delivering fuel in major congested expressways. This comes after the Supreme National Security Council told nearly 10 million residents to leave the capital. Iranian Red Crescent spokesperson Mojtaba Khaledi said at least 20 of Iran’s 32 provinces have been targeted. Citizens are urged to stay away from points of impact. The government has emphasised that it has enough food and fuel, and ministers and governors of border provinces have been given additional authorities to import essential goods when necessary. Universities and schools are closed until further notice, and classes will be moved online when possible. Many universities and schools had already been taken online over recent weeks amid student protests.

title: Qatar’s priority is de-escalation: Analyst
content: The current priority for Qatar is de-escalation, Rashid al-Mohanadi, a nonresident fellow at the Middle East Council on Global Affairs, has told Al Jazeera. “I don’t see a diplomatic off-rank forming anytime soon … we might see in the coming days, depends on how intensive the strikes are on both sides”, he said.

title: Dismay in Geneva as Iran attacks derail nuclear talks
content: Just a few hours ago, Oman’s Foreign Minister Badr bin Hamad Albusaidi was talking to US media about the real possibility of a deal on Iran’s nuclear programme. There has been a lot of dismay here in Geneva, where the talks were being held, and quite a few European nations have reacted, calling for de-escalation and restraint. Iran had been warning about this, and its diplomats had told me that if a war broke out, Iran would not be showing restraint as it did in previous rounds. What we are seeing now are attacks on US military personnel in the region, but we still have not seen reactions from Iranian-allied groups in places including Yemen and Iraq.

title: White House says no Trump address scheduled as of yet
content: Reports saying Trump will deliver an address this morning are inaccurate, the White House says, adding that it has not given any guidance to the media on an address or remarks today.

title: New missile interceptions seen in Doha
content: A new round of missile interceptions could be seen in the Qatari capital, Doha. An Al Jazeera reporter counted at least five blasts. White trails could be seen in the sky.

title: US-Israel have launched a ‘war of opportunity’
content: Ali Vaez, the Iran project director at Crisis Group, has told Al Jazeera the US-Israel attack appears to be an attempt to seize on the perception that Iran has been severely weakened in recent years. “This is a war of choice. This is a war of opportunity, because Israel and the United States believe that the Islamic Republic is in a position of historic weakness,” he said. Vaez noted that the US appears to want full capitulation from Iran, adding that “from the Iranian perspective, [this] is even more dangerous than suffering another US and Israeli strike.” “There’s a lot of wishful thinking that by destabilising the Iranian regime, something better will come into place. But of course, we’ve seen this movie before in that part of the world. US regime change policies often end in grief,” he added.

title: Radar system in northern Qatar targeted by Iranian strike
content: A Qatari Defence Ministry official told Al Jazeera that an early-warning radar in northern Qatar was targeted in an Iranian attack.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah, occupied West Bank
title: Explosions heard in Ramallah as Israel intercepts incoming Iranian attacks
content: From where we are here in Ramallah, we can hear loud explosions of Israel’s defence system intercepting Iranian missiles. Residents in Ramallah rely on Israeli sirens heard in the distance to know that there are incoming attacks, even though there are no bunkers in Palestinian homes. So far, there have been no reports of injuries in Israel, even though on social media we have seen reports of direct impacts. But we have very little footage of what is happening because the Israeli military has been very clear that reports and videos of direct impact are not allowed. Certainly, we had not seen before this kind of intensity in sirens going off and warnings to take shelter for such prolonged periods. Israelis and Palestinians understand this is like nothing they’ve seen before.

title: Israel’s energy minister expected to declare a state of emergency in the natural gas sector: Report
content: Israeli newspaper Haaretz is reporting that as Israel “braces for Iranian missile attacks”, its energy sector “has begun shifting into emergency mode, with refinery operations scaled back and parts of its natural gas infrastructure temporarily halted”. It reported that Israel’s Bazan Group said it has partially shut down its Haifa refinery facilities, and Israel’s Energy Minister Eli Cohen has ordered a temporary halt to some offshore gas platforms and is expected to declare a state of emergency in the natural gas sector.

title: ‘This is going to be catastrophic’ for US, Iran and region
content: Negar Mortazavi, senior fellow at the Center for International Policy, says Trump is betraying his campaign promises by launching another regime change war in the region. Mortazavi stressed that conflict could have been averted, saying that negotiations between Washington and Tehran were making progress before the US-Israeli attack. “This is going to be catastrophic for the United States, for Iranians and for people across the region,” she told Al Jazeera. “As we’ve already seen on day one, the war is spilling over and is harming others.” She added that Israel has been pushing the US and appears to be getting the conflict it has sought for years. “This is another Israeli war that the US is launching.”

title: Iran’s foreign minister says strike on girls’ school will ‘not go unanswered’
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has said the attack on a girls’ elementary school in Minab, a city in the Hormozgan province of southern Iran, will “not go unanswered.” According to state-run IRNA news outlet, the attack killed 53 students. Araghchi said the victims were all “innocent children”. The destroyed building is a primary school for girls in the south of Iran. It was bombed in broad daylight, when packed with young pupils. Dozens of innocent children have been murdered at this site alone. These crimes against the Iranian People will not go unanswered. pic.twitter.com/AVqiuolgWm — Seyed Abbas Araghchi (@araghchi) February 28, 2026

title: Iranian government confident it has withstood the first wave of attacks
content: The government here is telling people that although this has been a very powerful attack, things are under control. They are reassuring people that now they can move if they want, they can travel from city to city to go to safer locations. They say provisions are in place; there is no need to panic. And also, they are talking about the failure of the attack so far, because the leadership hasn’t been touched, as they say here in the official reports. The bet [that the Americans and Israelis made] that the people opposed to the regime will go into the streets and try to change the regime, we haven’t seen any signs of that.

title: Iranian attacks on Israel and US bases to continue: IRGC
content: A source in Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has said Iranian attacks on Israel, as well as on US bases and interests across the region, will “continue unabated”, Fars news agency is reporting.

title: US senator says Trump’s attack on Iran ‘illegal and unconstitutional’
content: US Senator Edward J Markey has called Trump’s military attack on Iran “illegal and unconstitutional”. “It was not approved by Congress and holds dangers for all Americans. Trump’s illegal actions raise the threat of escalation into a wider regional war with grave risks for U.S. troops and civilians in the region. Trump has consistently exaggerated the imminence of Iran’s nuclear threat, even after insisting the United States ‘obliterated’ Iran’s nuclear program during his illegal Operation Midnight Hammer attack,” he said in a statement. “Even Secretary of State Marco Rubio admitted Iran is not enriching uranium. There was time for diplomacy before this attack, and there still is,” Markey added, calling for a diplomatic solution. “This is a crisis of Trump’s creation. Americans do not want another endless war in the Middle East. They do not want to see young men and women sent overseas for endless warfare while families at home cannot afford health care. If Trump does not stop this war now, Congress must. No more war with Iran.”

title: Explosion heard in Iran’s southeastern city of Chabahar
content: Iran’s state media is reporting that an explosion has been heard in the country’s southeastern port city of Chabahar.

title: Oman calls for UN Security Council meeting
content: Oman’s Foreign Ministry warned the US-Israel attacks on Iran risk “the danger of the conflict expanding into consequences that cannot be rectified in the region”. It further called on the UN Security Council to convene an urgent meeting to “impose a ceasefire and for the international community to take a clear stance in support of international law”. It added that it affirmed the “right to states’ self-defence” in accordance with international law, while urging restraint and diplomacy. Oman had been serving as a mediator in indirect US-Iran talks before the attacks.

title: Bahrain switches schools to distance learning
content: Bahrain has announced its schools will switch to distance learning “until further notice”. Bahrain is one of the regional countries that has been targeted by Iranian retaliation after it was targeted by US and Israeli attacks.

title: Iranian agricultural minister says food preparation complete to avoid shortages
content: The Iranian minister of agriculture has announced an uninterrupted and complete supply and preparation of food items amid attacks on the country, Tasnim news agency reported. According to the report, all necessary measures and forecasts have been made, so there are no shortages, especially in light of the Islamic holy month of Ramadan and the Persian New Year on March 20.

title: Saudi Arabia’s MBS expresses ‘full solidarity’ with UAE: State media
content: Saudi Arabia’s Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has held a phone call with United Arab Emirates President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan to discuss the recent Iranian attacks, UAE’s state news agency WAM reported. “During the call, they discussed developments in the region and the blatant Iranian attacks that targeted the territory of the United Arab Emirates and a number of sisterly countries,” it added. “His royal highness, the crown prince of Saudi Arabia, expressed the kingdom’s condemnation of the attacks, its full solidarity and support for the United Arab Emirates, and its readiness to place all its capabilities at the disposal of the UAE in all the measures it takes.”

title: This campaign against Iran very different from the one we saw in June 2025
content: There has been some calm over the last couple of hours, but there have been very many intense strikes this morning, including here in the capital, Tehran, particularly the Pasteur district. That’s a place where you have more government and military institutions, and also the presidential palace and the homes of some of the government’s leadership. Across the country, many cities, probably up to 10 cities, have been targeted in all corners of Iran, and the Americans and Israelis have targeted military sites, strategic locations, but we haven’t heard much of a focus on the nuclear sites during this campaign today, and that matches the motto of this new campaign. It’s not just [about] the nuclear capabilities of Iran, but also weakening the regime and taking out the missile capabilities of the country. US President Trump has promised that. So now, we are seeing a campaign that is different from the one we saw in June.

title: Some oil companies suspend fuel shipments in Strait of Hormuz: Reports
content: Amid the attacks in the region, some top oil and trading companies have suspended shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, according to reports. “Our ships will stay ‌put for several days,” one top executive ⁠at a major trading company told the Reuters news agency. About 20 million barrels of crude oil and other fuel types travel through the arterial waterway, which passes between the Arabian Peninsula and Iran, with any suspensions threatening major global disruptions.

title: Drone targets Erbil International Airport
content: Our correspondent is reporting that Erbil International Airport in the Kurdish region of northern Iraq has been targeted again. The latest incident involved a drone which air defences intercepted and shot down.

title: Large swath of US Congress in support of attack on Iran
content: Congress is aware of what happened overnight. The administration briefed the so-called “gang of eight”, that is, the Democrats, as well as the Republican leadership, in both the House of Representatives, the lower chamber of the US Congress and the Senate. That message has already been disseminated. So it’s no surprise to the Congress. Add to that, there was at least a large swath of the Congress that was very supportive of this and had been pushing for conflict with Iran. As recently as last Tuesday, Marco Rubio, the secretary of state and also the national security adviser for the president, was on Capitol Hill, briefing the members on Iran.

title: Multiple Arab states that host US assets targeted in Iran retaliation
content: Iran has targeted US assets across the Gulf Arab states in retaliation for a huge joint attack on Iran by the US and Israel, as the region’s worst fears of being ignited in the flames of a sustained war loom. The Iranian government on Saturday confirmed its attacks on several targets , according to the Fars news agency, including Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates, where US airbases are hosted. Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claimed all Israeli and US military targets in the Middle East have been struck “by the powerful blows of Iranian missiles”. Read more here . Smoke rises in the sky after blasts were heard in Manama, Bahrain, February 28, 2026. [REUTERS]

title: Photos: Aftermath of the strike on a girls’ school in Iran
content: [Screengrab/IRIB TV via AFP] [Screengrab/IRIB TV via AFP] [Screengrab/IRIB TV via AFP]

title: Kuwait says ballistic missiles targeting airbase intercepted
content: Kuwait’s Defence Ministry says that Ali al-Salem Air Base came under attack by a number of ballistic missiles, all of which were intercepted by Kuwaiti air defence systems. “Ali Al-Salem Air Base came under attack by a number of ballistic missiles. This morning, the Kuwaiti Air Defense Force successfully intercepted them, resulting in fragments and debris from the interception operation falling in the vicinity of the base,” ministry’s spokesperson, Colonel Staff Saud Abdulaziz al-Otaibi, said in a statement. An aerial view taken with a drone shows Kuwait Telecommunication Tower and the surrounding in Kuwait City, Kuwait City, Kuwait [File: Stephanie McGehee/Reuters]

title: Latest developments
content: A US official has told Al Jazeera that it will continue to launch “extensive” strikes from air and sea at Iran, as Israeli leader says attacks aimed to remove “existential threat”. Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has said Israeli and US military targets in the Middle East have been struck “by the powerful blows of Iranian missiles”. It added the operation would continue “relentlessly”. Attacks have been reported on bases hosting US troops across the region, with explosions and missile interceptions reported in Qatar, the UAE, Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait. Oman’s Foreign Ministry has appealed to US, saying “this is not your war”, while the emir of Qatar, Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani and Saudi Arabia’s Prince Mohammed bin Salman urged an “immediate halt to the escalation” during a call.

title: Iran targeting of Gulf states can have massive geopolitical impact
content: Twenty percent of the world’s energy goes through the Strait of Hormuz, as does much international trade in this region. The Middle East, especially the Gulf countries, are major centres of economic activity, financial markets, all kinds of investment, and the movement of people. These are bustling cities with bustling marketplaces, and the majority of the population in much of the GCC are expat workers who come here to live and work. So it is certainly a major world marketplace that is also currently under attack. I don’t think it is an overstatement to say that what is going to happen now is perhaps a massive shift – economically, geopolitically. This could be the beginning of major changes in the region. What that looks like, how things are when the guns fall silent – if they do soon enough – is still an open question.
