title: Qatar, Saudi Arabia urge ‘immediate halt’ to escalations during call
content: Qatar’s emir has spoken to Saudi Arabia’s crown prince and exchanged views on the ongoing developments and their impacts on regional and international security. According to a readout of the call, Qatar and Saudi Arabia stressed the need for an “immediate halt “to any escalations and a return to the negotiation table. Saudi Arabia affirmed its “full solidarity and support for the State of Qatar, its strong condemnation of the targeting of Qatari territory with Iranian ballistic missiles, and its commitment to providing all possible support to the State of Qatar in all measures it takes to preserve its sovereignty, security and stability”. Qatar’s emir expressed his “deep gratitude and appreciation for the firm stances of the sisterly Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, and for its sincere fraternal solidarity and unwavering support for the State of Qatar and its people”.

title: UAE intercepts new wave of missiles
content: UAE’s Defence Ministry says it has intercepted a new wave of missiles. There have also been reports of explosions heard in Dubai, which might have been caused by the interceptions.

title: Israeli army warns of incoming ‘barrage of missiles’
content: The Israeli military has issued a statement warning of an incoming “barrage of missiles” and instructing residents to take shelter. It came minutes after an earlier notice warned that Israel’s defence system was working to intercept incoming threats.

title: At least 51 people killed in Israeli attack on school in southern Iran: State media
content: The number of people killed in the Israeli attack on the girls’ elementary school in Iran’s southern city of Minab has risen to 51, with 60 others injured, according to the IRNA news agency. Mohammad Radmehr, a city official, was quoted by the agency as saying that Israel “directly attacked” the school this morning. He said 170 students were in the facility when the strike happened, so there was a possibility that the death toll would rise again as the rescue operation was still under way.

title: Russia’s Rosatom says nuclear sites in Iran should not be targeted ‘under any circumstances’
content: Russian state nuclear corporation Rosatom says it has evacuated 94 people from Iran, but its personnel remain at the Bushehr nuclear power plant. Quoting CEO Alexei Likhachev, Rosatom said it had evacuated all employees’ children, as well as “non-essential staff” and anyone who wished to leave the country, as Israel and the US continue to target Iran. “We are constantly monitoring the situation and assessing the risks. If necessary, additional measures will be taken in cooperation with the Ministry of Foreign Affairs to ensure the safety of our employees,” Likhachev said. Likhachev did not elaborate on the situation at Bushehr but said that nuclear facilities “should not be targeted under any circumstances”. Bushehr was built by Russia and is Iran’s only operational nuclear power plant.

title: Iran deputy foreign minister says timing of attacks ‘deeply troubling’
content: Hamid Ghanbari, Iran’s deputy foreign minister, says the timing of today’s US-Israeli strikes on Iran during a new round of nuclear talks is “deeply troubling”. Such timing “breaks trust and raises serious doubt about the commitment to a peaceful solution”, Ghanbari said in an interview with Al Jazeera. Ghanbari added that Iran has the right to defend itself and regrets any humanitarian loss caused by the current military escalation.

title: Iranian foreign minister says armed forces ‘will teach aggressors lesson they deserve’
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has released a defiant message on social media. “Netanyahu and Trump’s war on Iran is wholly unprovoked, illegal, and illegitimate,” he said, adding “Trump has turned ‘America First’ into ‘Israel First’ – which always means ‘America Last’.” Araghchi added: “Our Powerful Armed Forces are prepared for this day and will teach the aggressors the lesson they deserve.” His message included a screenshot of a 2012 tweet by Trump saying: “Now that Obama’s poll numbers are in tailspin – watch for him to launch a strike in Libya or Iran. He is desperate.” Netanyahu and Trump's war on Iran is wholly unprovoked, illegal, and illegitimate. Trump has turned 'America First' into 'Israel First'—which always means 'America Last'. Our Powerful Armed Forces are prepared for this day and will teach the aggressors the lesson they deserve pic.twitter.com/xhiWydMyXy — Seyed Abbas Araghchi (@araghchi) February 28, 2026

title: UN urges all parties to ‘see reason’
content: The United Nations rights chief deplored the strikes in the Middle East and urged all parties to return to negotiations, saying attacks would only result in “death, destruction and human misery”. “I deplore the military strikes across Iran this morning by Israel and the United States of America, and the subsequent retaliatory strikes by Iran,” Volker Turk said in a statement. “As always, in any armed conflict, it is civilians who end up paying the ultimate price. Turk said missiles and bombs were “not the way to resolve differences” but will only “result in death, destruction and human misery”. “To avert these terrible consequences for civilians, I call for restraint and implore all parties to see reason, to de-escalate, and for a return to the negotiating table where they had been actively seeking a solution only hours earlier,” he said. Qatari capital Doha after missiles fired by Iran were intercepted [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera]

title: Sirens sound across Bahrain
content: Bahrain’s Interior Ministry says sirens are sounding across the country.

title: US attacks Iran despite claims it ‘obliterated’ Iran’s nuclear program
content: The White House has repeatedly insisted that its June 2025 strikes against Iran had destroyed the country’s nuclear programme. “We wiped it out and they want to start all over again. And they’re at this moment again pursuing their sinister ambitions,” Trump said during his annual State of the Union address on Tuesday. White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt also said earlier this week that the attack “did, in fact, obliterate Iran’s nuclear facilities”. Iran repeatedly said it would not pursue nuclear weapons, but maintained the right to enrich uranium for civil purposes.

title: Falling rocket debris in Doha’s outskirts causes panic
content: Video footage circulated online shows dramatic scenes following falling missile debris on the outskirts of Qatar’s capital, Doha. The footage, verified by Al Jazeera, shows people screaming and scrambling towards safety as the object falls from the sky, leading to a massive explosion, resulting in red flames and thick black smoke. “It is right next to my room,” one person is heard saying of the explosion. “Oh my God! May Allah protect us,” he adds. From Qatar pic.twitter.com/J59oa3VELj — Sleepless in Lahore (@mahobili) February 28, 2026

title: Russia’s Lavrov condemns strikes on Iran in call with Iranian minister
content: Russia’s ⁠Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov held ⁠a phone call ⁠with his Iranian counterpart Abbas Araghchi, where he condemned what Russia’s Foreign Ministry said was ⁠an “unprovoked armed attack” ⁠by the US and ⁠Israel on Iran, ⁠and urged ⁠an immediate halt to the strikes.

title: Dubai airport suspends all flight operations
content: Dubai airport has suspended all flight operations until further notice.

title: Israel’s air defence systems
Explosions periodically rock Qatar as air defences intercept Iranian missiles
content: Explosions continue to be heard periodically in Qatar’s capital as the air defence continues to intercept Iranian missiles. This latest series of blasts comes after multiple missiles were fired towards Qatar and other Gulf states. Smoke was seen billowing from a residential area on the outskirts of the capital, caused by falling debris from an intercepted missile.

title: Spain’s prime minister rejects ‘unilateral’ military action by US, Israel
content: Spain’s ⁠Prime Minister Sanchez has ⁠rejected what he called ⁠”unilateral” military action by the US and Israel, which he ‌said represented an escalation and contributed to a more uncertain and hostile international order. In a ⁠statement posted on ⁠social media platform X, Sanchez also said he ⁠rejected the actions of ⁠the Iranian ⁠government and the Revolutionary Guard. “We demand immediate de-escalation ‌and full respect for international law,” he ‌added.

title: Israel bans public gatherings, shuts schools
content: Israel has banned ⁠public gatherings, shut schools ⁠and workplaces and moved hospital patients to underground facilities. Defense Minister Israel Katz declared a ⁠state of emergency across the country, warning the public of Iranian missile and drone attacks. The police urged against non-essential travel to allow security and emergency vehicles to move freely.

title: ‘This is not your war’: Omani foreign minister urges US ‘not to get sucked in further’
content: We have the first public reaction by Badr Albusaidi, the foreign minister of Oman, who had been mediating the recent rounds of indirect talks between the US and Iran. In a social media post, Albusaidi said he was “dismayed” by today’s attacks. “Active and serious negotiations have yet again been undermined. Neither the interests of the United States nor the cause of global peace are well served by this. And I pray for the innocents who will suffer,” he added. “I urge the United States not to get sucked in further. This is not your war.” I am dismayed. Active and serious negotiations have yet again been undermined. Neither the interests of the United States nor the cause of global peace are well served by this. And I pray for the innocents who will suffer. I urge the United States not to get sucked in further.… — Badr Albusaidi – بدر البوسعيدي (@badralbusaidi) February 28, 2026

title: Photos: Smoke over Doha as Qatar intercepts more Iranian missiles
content: [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera] [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera] [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera] [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera]

title: How has Iran responded?
content: Shortly after the US and Israel struck Iran, explosions were heard in northern Israel as it sought to shoot down incoming Iranian missiles. The detonations were heard soon after the Israeli army announced it was deploying air defence systems to counter the Iranian barrage. There were no immediate reports of casualties or damage from the continuing assault. Blasts were also reported in several Gulf Arab countries hosting US military forces, including Qatar, Kuwait, the UAE and Bahrain. Iran’s Fars News Agency stated that Tehran launched strikes on military installations in each of those states, naming Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar and the US Navy 5th Fleet headquarters in Bahrain among the targets. People inspecting the damage at an impact site following US and Israeli strikes on Tehran [Photo by various sources/AFP]

title: UAE condemns Iranian strikes, warns of ‘grave consequences’
content: The UAE has condemned Iranian missile attacks that killed a Pakistani national earlier, warning of “grave consequences” if these violations continue. The civilian was killed after being struck by falling debris, according to the Defence Ministry. In a statement, the Foreign Ministry said any attack on Gulf nations was “an attack on the security and stability of the entire region”. It called for restraint and for a path towards a diplomatic solution.

title: Two students killed in strike on school near Tehran
content: Iran’s Mehr news agency is reporting that at least two students have been killed by an attack that hit a school east of the capital, Tehran. Earlier, an Israeli strike hit an elementary girls’ school in Minab, a city in the Hormozgan province of southern Iran, killing 40 people, state media reported.

title: Macron says ‘dangerous escalation must stop’
content: We have just received a statement from Emmanuel Macron, the president of France, who says the outbreak of war between the US, Israel and Iran “has grave consequences for international peace and security”. “The current escalation is dangerous for everyone. It must stop,” he said. “At this crucial moment, all measures are being taken to ensure the security of our national territory, our citizens, and our assets in the Middle East,” added Macron, noting that France stands ready to deploy “the necessary resources to protect its closest partners, should they request it”.

title: ‘Dozens of drones’ launched towards Israel: Iran army
content: Iran’s army says a wave of attack drones has been launched towards Israel, according to the Tasnim news agency. The military statement cited by the agency said it was the first large drone offensive carried out by Iran against Israel.

title: Emirates airlines suspends operations at Dubai airport
content: Emirates airlines says operations from Dubai airport are temporarily suspended due to airspace closure in the region.

title: Israel operating for ‘remote standoff’ with no troops in Iran
content: Andreas Krieg, associate professor in security studies at King’s College London, says there is a “key element” missing from Israel’s and the US’s plans for regime changes, which includes troops on the ground. “The Israelis think they can mobilise a very alienated, aggrieved Iranian population to take action against their own regime. I don’t see any way that can actually happen because there isn’t a coherent, consolidated opposition or weapons that are being distributed,” Krieg told Al Jazeera. “So basically this is a remote standoff, airpower-led campaign that can maybe lead to degradation. But actually, without seizing ground and holding it, there is no way the regime is just going to collapse and disappear.” Krieg explained that the US will “run out of steam” after it completes its target lists, “which can likely come in a few days”.

title: Middle East attacks igniting ‘dangerous chain reaction’: Red Cross chief
content: The military escalation in the Middle East is prompting a dangerous chain reaction across the entire region, the head of the International Committee of the Red Cross warns. Mirjana Spoljaric, ICRC president, called on countries to respect the rules of war and urged them to find the political will to prevent “further death and destruction”. “The military escalation in the Middle East is igniting a dangerous chain reaction across the region, with potentially devastating consequences for civilians,” Spoljaric said in a statement. “Upholding the rules of war is an obligation and not a choice. In international armed conflicts, international humanitarian law, in particular all four Geneva Conventions, apply. “Civilian infrastructure such as hospitals, homes and schools must be spared from attack. Medical personnel and first responders must be allowed to carry out their work safely,” Spoljaric said. “The military escalation in the Middle East is igniting a dangerous chain reaction across the region, with potentially devastating consequences for civilians.” Mirjana Spoljaric, ICRC president, urges respect for rules of war. Read more 👉🏼 https://t.co/HqkzVzkVJ5 pic.twitter.com/imkkgbEK6F — ICRC (@ICRC) February 28, 2026

title: Israeli army says it hit ‘hundreds of Iranian military targets’
content: Israel’s military has issued a statement claiming to have hit “hundreds of Iranian military targets”, including missile launchers, as it conducted air attacks on western Iran. It added that its air force was working to intercept incoming retaliatory attacks from Iran.

title: What have Israel and Netanyahu said?
content: PM Netanyahu says the joint US‑Israel strikes on Iran aim to remove an “existential threat”. “The time has come for all sections of the people in Iran … to remove the yoke of tyranny … and bring a free and peace-loving Iran,” Netanyahu said in a statement. “Our joint operation will create the conditions for the brave Iranian people to take their fate into their own hands.”

title: Death toll after Israeli attack on school in Iran jumps to 40: State media
content: We have reported earlier that an Israeli strike hit an elementary girls’ school in Minab, a city in the Hormozgan province of southern Iran, killing 24 people. The death toll now jumped to 40, according to the state-run IRNA news agency.

title: What did Trump say about the attacks?
content: President Trump stated that the joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran are for “eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime”. Here is what he has said so far: “Our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating threats from the Iranian regime.” “We are going to destroy their missiles and raze their missile industry to the ground.” “We are going to annihilate their navy. We are going to ensure that the region’s terrorist proxies can no longer destabilise the region or the world.” “We will ensure that Iran does not obtain a nuclear weapon. It is a very simple message.” “They will never have a nuclear weapon. This regime will soon learn that no one should challenge the strength and might of the United States Armed Forces.”

title: Iran’s senior officials ‘in perfect health’, local media claims
content: The Fars news agency says Iran’s senior officials are “in perfect health”, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and security chief Ali Larijani. “While Israeli sources have claimed to have targeted and succeeded in attacking the president and senior Iranian leaders, official sources within the country report that senior regime officials, including the heads of the armed forces, are in perfect health,” the report said.

title: Timeline: Talks and threats leading up to US, Israel attacks
content: The strikes came just two days after high-stakes US-Iran nuclear negotiations in Geneva, mediated by Oman, ended without a breakthrough. The US-Israel attack marks the most serious escalation since the brief but intense June 2025 war. Check out our story here for a timeline of the events, including attacks and diplomatic overtures, leading up to these strikes by the US and Israel.

title: Why did the US and Israel attack Iran?
content: US and Israeli attacks on Iran are rooted in years of confrontation over Tehran’s nuclear programme and regional influence. The two allies have long argued that Iran’s advancing enrichment activities and missile capabilities pose an existential threat and have repeatedly threatened to use force to stop it. In June last year, Israel and the US attacked Iranian nuclear and military sites, including assassinations of senior commanders and strikes on facilities inside Iran. Iranian officials, including President Masoud Pezeshkian, have repeatedly said they will never develop nuclear weapons. US intelligence agencies and the UN nuclear watchdog have not found evidence that Iran is actively seeking an atomic weapon, yet Israel and some figures in the Trump administration have continued to promote that claim.

title: US to continue launching ‘extensive’ air and sea strikes
content: A US official has told Al Jazeera that it will continue to launch “extensive” strikes from the air and sea at Iran. The US-Israeli attacks aim to dismantle the Iranian security apparatus and are currently limited to targets in Iran, the official said. They confirmed that the US Air Force was participating in the strikes and that it was coordinating with the Israeli military.

title: Aftermath of US-Israeli attack on Khamenei’s secure compound
content: [Screengrab/Al Jazeera]

title: Philippine president says safety of citizens in region of ‘paramount’ importance
content: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr says the safety of Filipino citizens in Iran and the Middle East is “paramount” following attacks. “I have instructed the DFA [Department of Foreign Affairs] and the DMW [Department of Migrant Workers] to take measures to determine their whereabouts in affected areas and to take immediate measures to ensure their safety,” he said in a post on X. According to the Department of Migrant Workers, there were an estimated 2.1 million Filipino workers across the Middle East in 2024.

title: WATCH: Moment of impact on US military base in Bahrain
More countries react to Israeli, US attacks on Iran
content: Belgium: “The Iranian people must not pay the price for their government’s choices,” Foreign Minister Maxime Prevot said. “We deeply regret that diplomatic efforts could not lead earlier to a negotiated solution.” Norway: “The attack is ⁠described by Israel as a preventive strike, but ⁠it is not in line with international law,” Foreign Minister Espen Barth said. “Preventive attacks require an immediately imminent threat.” Pakistan: Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar “strongly condemned the unwarranted attacks against Iran and called for an immediate halt to escalation through urgent resumption of diplomacy to achieve a peaceful, negotiated resolution to the crisis”.

title: Iraqi group threatens imminent attacks on US bases
content: Kataib Hezbollah, an Iraqi paramilitary group, says it will soon begin attacking US bases in response to the attacks that targeted the group. Kataib Hezbollah is one of the largest groups within the Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF), founded in 2014 to stop lightning advances by ISIL (ISIS) at the time. Two fighters were killed in air strikes that hit the Jurf al-Sakher base, also known as Jurf al-Nasr, in southern Iraq.

title: Photos: Aftermath of US and Israeli strikes in Tehran
content: [Screengrab via AFP] [Screengrab via AFP]

title: Number of students killed in Israeli attack on school in southern Iran rises to 24
content: We have reported earlier that an Israeli strike hit an elementary girls’ school in Minab, a city in the Hormozgan province of southern Iran, killing five people. Now, Iran’s Fars news agency is reporting that the number of students killed rose to 24.

title: Bahrain Interior Ministry evacuating Juffair residents
content: Bahrain’s Interior Ministry says it has started evacuating people residing in the Juffair area, where a US naval base is hosted. As we previously reported, smoke was seen rising over the area. “We urge your cooperation with the relevant authorities,” the ministry said, The Ministry of Interior has begun evacuating citizens and residents in the Juffair area. We urge your cooperation with the relevant authorities. pic.twitter.com/SKjGJB6sT2 — Ministry of Interior (@moi_bahrain) February 28, 2026

title: Indonesia says President Prabowo ready to facilitate US-Iran dialogue
content: Indonesia’s Foreign Ministry says President Prabowo Subianto is prepared to mediate between Washington and Tehran. “Indonesia deeply regrets the failure of negotiations between US and Iran, which has resulted in military escalation in the Middle East. Indonesia calls on all parties to exercise restraint and to prioritise dialogue and diplomacy,” the Foreign Ministry wrote on X. “The Government of Indonesia, particularly the President of the Republic of Indonesia, expresses his readiness to facilitate dialogue to re-establish a conducive security condition and if agreed by both parties, the President of Indonesia is willing to travel to Tehran to conduct mediation,” the post read, adding that tensions had the potential to disrupt the region and “world peace and security”. Indonesia deeply regrets the failure of negotiations between the US and Iran, which has resulted in military escalation in the Middle East. Indonesia calls on all parties to exercise restraint and to prioritize dialogue and diplomacy. Indonesia re-emphasizes the importance of… — MoFA Indonesia (@Kemlu_RI) February 28, 2026

title: Russia urges immediate halt to US, Israeli attacks on Iran
content: We also have a reaction from Russia, which has called for an immediate stop to US and ⁠Israeli attacks on Iran, saying ⁠the situation must be “returned to the path of political and diplomatic settlement”. In a statement, the ‌Foreign Ministry said the international community should swiftly deliver an objective assessment of what it called irresponsible ⁠actions that risk ⁠further destabilising the region. It added that it remains ready, ⁠as before, to ⁠assist in efforts to ⁠find peaceful solutions based on international law, mutual respect ‌and a balance of interests.

title: Iranian missiles target Israel again, efforts under way to intercept: Army
content: The Israeli army is trying to intercept a new wave of Iranian missiles that were launched “a short while ago”, the military statement says. “A short while ago, sirens were sounded in several areas across the country following the identification of missiles launched from Iran toward the State of Israel,” it said. “At this time, the Israeli Air Force is operating to intercept and strike threats where necessary to remove the threat.” “The defense is not hermetic, and therefore it is essential that the public continue to adhere to the Home Front Command’s guidelines. The public is requested to continue to follow the instructions of the Home Front Command,” the alert said.

title: Israel says some residents allowed to leave protected spaces
content: The Israeli army says citizens in several areas across Israel are allowed to leave protected spaces. It added in a statement on Telegram that search and rescue teams were operating across the country.

title: Which airlines have cancelled flights to the region?
content: Air France Air India British Airways Iberia Express Indigo Japan Airlines LOT Airlines Lufthansa Norwegian Air Turkish Airlines Virgin Atlantic Qatar Airways Air Algerie Scandinavian Airlines Wizz Air Pegasus Airlines ITA Airways

title: Iran’s National Security Council tells residents to leave Tehran
content: Iran’s National Security Council has advised residents to leave the capital, Tehran. Because of the situation, “you should, as far as possible and while maintaining calm, travel to other places and cities if you are able,” the country’s security body said in a statement.

title: Kuwait condemns Iranian attack, says it reserves right to respond
content: Kuwait’s Foreign Ministry has condemned attacks on its soil, which it said were conducted by Iran “in flagrant violation” of its airspace and of international law. It said Kuwait reserves the right to respond in a manner that is “commensurate with the scale and nature of this attack” and warned that further military activity would undermine the region’s stability. A drone view shows Kuwait City [Stephanie McGehee/Reuters]

title: Video shows smoke over Doha suburb
content: Footage verified by Al Jazeera shows smoke billowing over a building in a residential area on the outskirts of Qatar’s capital, Doha. No casualties were reported. View this post on Instagram

title: WATCH: Netanyahu says US, Israel attacked Iran to remove ‘existential threat’
Explosion reported in northwest Iran
content: Iran’s IRNA news agency is reporting an explosion in Zanjan, northwest Iran.

title: IRGC says Iran’s military operation to continue ‘until the enemy is decisively defeated’
content: Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claims all Israeli and US military targets in the Middle East have been struck “by the powerful blows of Iranian missiles”. “This operation will continue relentlessly until the enemy is decisively defeated,” it said. All US assets throughout the region are considered legitimate targets for Iran’s army, it added.

title: X
content: We are getting reports that Trump could deliver an address to the nation. We’re following this and bring any lines as soon as it’s confirmed.

title: EU’s Von der Leyen, Costa call for ‘maximum restraint’
content: European ⁠Commission President ⁠Ursula von der Leyen and European Council ⁠President Antonio Costa have said ⁠developments in Iran are “greatly concerning”. “We call ⁠on all parties to exercise maximum restraint, to protect civilians, ‌and to fully respect international law,” the two ⁠leaders said in a ⁠joint statement. “In close coordination with EU member states, we will take all necessary steps to ensure that ⁠EU citizens in ⁠the region can count on our full support.”

title: Qatar condemns Iranian missile attack, says it reserves right to respond
content: We have a new statement from Qatar’s Foreign Ministry saying the country reserves the right of response following the Iranian attack, in according with international law. It said Qatar strongly condemned the targeting of Qatari territory with ballistic missiles, considering it a flagrant violation of its national sovereignty, a direct assault on its security and an unacceptable escalation that threatens the security and stability of the region. Doha’s skyline after Iranian missiles were intercepted [Sorin Furcoi/Al Jazeera]

title: Safety of residents, visitors top priority, says Dubai’s media office
content: Dubai’s media office says it is continuing to operate with the safety of citizens and visitors as its “top priority” following Iranian attacks.

title: British Airways cancels Tel Aviv, Bahrain flights
content: British Airways has cancelled flights to Tel ⁠Aviv and Bahrain until March 3. “We are closely monitoring the situation and have taken the operational decision to cancel our ⁠flights to Tel ⁠Aviv and Bahrain up to and including 03 March and ⁠have cancelled today’s service to Amman,” ⁠the airline said ⁠in a statement. “Safety is always our top priority, and ‌we’re contacting our customers to advise them of their ‌travel ‌options.”

title: Explosions in Tel Aviv
content: Explosions have now been reported from Tel Aviv. We will bring you more as soon as we can.

title: New wave of Iranian missiles target Israel: Army
content: Israel’s army says sirens were sounded “a short while ago” in several areas across the country following the identification of missiles launched from Iran. “At this time, the Israeli Air Force is operating to intercept and strike threats where necessary to remove the threat,” the military statement said on Telegram. “The defense is not hermetic, and therefore it is essential that the public continue to adhere to the Home Front Command’s guidelines. The public is requested to continue to follow the instructions of the Home Front Command,” it added.

title: More explosions heard in Qatar’s capital Doha
content: New explosions have been heard in Doha. This comes shortly after Qatar’s Defense Ministry said it had downed all missiles targeting the country in a “second wave” of attacks.

title: Saudi Arabia condemns attacks on Bahrain, Qatar, Jordan and Kuwait
content: Saudi Arabia has condemned in the “strongest terms” the Iranian attacks on the UAE, Bahrain, Qatar, Jordan and Kuwait. Saudi Arabia warned of “dire consequences” over the continued violation of “state sovereignty and the principles of international law”.

title: Five killed in Israeli attack on elementary school in Iran
content: Iran’s state-run IRNA news agency has reported that an Israeli strike hit an elementary girls’ school in Minab, a city in the Hormozgan province of southern Iran. It added that five students were killed.

title: UK says Iran ‘must never be allowed’ nuclear weapons
content: The UK reiterates that Iran must never be allowed to develop nuclear weapons, as the Foreign Office calls for British nationals in Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar and the UAE to immediately shelter.

title: Ukraine’s Foreign Ministry blames Iran government for attacks
content: Ukraine’s ⁠Foreign Ministry has said the current ⁠escalation in Iran was the ⁠fault of authorities in the country, adding that Kyiv stood with the ‌Iranian people and repeating calls for a change of government there. “The cause of the current ⁠events is precisely ⁠the violence and impunity of the Iranian regime, ⁠in particular the killings and ⁠repression of ⁠peaceful protesters, which have become particularly widespread in recent ‌months,” the Foreign Ministry said in a ‌statement.

title: At least one man injured after nine-storey building hit in northern Israel
content: A nine-storey building has been hit in northern Israel after Iranian missiles had been intercepted, leaving one man with minor injuries after he was struck by shrapnel from interceptor missiles, according to Israel’s Channel 12 broadcaster. The report added that extensive damage was sustained to one of the apartments. Israel’s Magen David Adom (MDA) emergency service said in a statement: “MDA paramedics and EMTs scanned several scenes where reports were received, and at this stage are providing medical treatment to a man approximately 50 years old in mild condition with blast injuries in northern Israel.”

title: Renewed explosions in Tehran an indication of attacks continuing
content: We can confirm that a little more than half hour ago, new sounds of explosions were heard here in Tehran. We couldn’t verify the locations because of the internet blackout and the lack of communications. But that’s an indication of the continuation of this operation – people earlier today were asking if this was just an attack or a wave of attacks, but now it’s clear, and it has also been expressed by the US side, that this is going to be a sustained operation. The Iranian Foreign Ministry, through its spokesperson, has said Iran has only good intentions towards its neighbours in the region, but has always said it is going to defend itself. Smoke rises following an explosion in Tehran [Majid Asgaripour/WANA via Reuters]

title: Who is Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei and why is he a possible target?
content: Among the areas targeted on Saturday in Iran’s capital Tehran were places linked to Iran’s Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei. Iran’s semiofficial Tasnim news agency reported that seven missiles hit an area near the presidential palace, located in Shemiran to the north of Tehran, as well as near Khamenei’s compound. The Associated Press reported that attacks also struck near Khamenei’s offices in the capital. The 86-year-old Islamic scholar has been Iran’s Supreme Leader since 1989, succeeding the Islamic Republic’s late founder, the charismatic Ayatollah Ruhollah Khomeini. Read more here .

title: Air India suspends Middle East flights
content: Air India has suspended all flights to the Middle East due to the ongoing situation.

title: Two Iraqi Popular Mobilisation Forces fighters killed in air strike south of Baghdad
content: At least two Iraqi Popular Mobilisation Forces (PMF) fighters have been killed and three seriously wounded following air strikes in Jurf al-Sakhar, south of Baghdad, a PMF spokesperson told the Reuters news agency. The PMF, also known in Arabic as Hashd al-Shaabi, is an umbrella group of mostly Shia paramilitary factions that includes several groups aligned with Iran.

title: Jordan downs two ballistic missiles
content: Two ballistic missiles have been shot down above Jordan, according to the country’s military. It was not clear who launched the missiles.

title: WATCH: Iran strikes US military base in Bahrain as explosions heard across Gulf states
EU’s Kallas says developments across Middle East ‘perilous’, calls to protect civilians
content: European Union foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas has described the latest developments in the Middle East as “perilous” and called for civilians to be protected and international law upheld. Kallas said she spoke to Israel’s Foreign Minister Gideon Saar and other ministers in the region and was coordinating with Arab partners to pursue a diplomatic path. The EU official added that the bloc’s consular network was coordinating departures for EU citizens as non-essential personnel were being withdrawn from the region.

title: UAE says it reserves ‘full right’ to respond to Iranian attack
content: The Emirati defence ministry has confirmed in a statement that the country was targeted by Iranian ballistic missiles, and air defences successfully intercepted a “number” of them. The statement read that shrapnel falling on a residential area in Abu Dhabi resulted in some material damage and the death of one person of Asian nationality. “The ministry condemned this attack in the strongest terms, stressing the state’s categorical rejection of targeting civilian objects, facilities and national institutions, and emphasising that such acts represent a dangerous escalation and a cowardly act that threatens the security and safety of civilians and undermines stability,” it said. “The ministry stressed that this targeting is a flagrant violation of national sovereignty and international law, and that the state reserves its full right to respond to this escalation and take all necessary measures to protect its territory,” the statement read, adding that it was “fully prepared and ready to deal with any threats.”

title: Bahrain terms attacks on its soil ‘treacherous’
content: Bahrain has confirmed that attacks targeted sites and facilities “in a blatant violation of the kingdom’s sovereignty and security”. It described them as “treacherous attacks” that pose a direct threat to Bahrain’s security and safety of its citizens and residents, Qatar’s news agency reported. Smoke rises in Manama, Bahrain [Reuters]

title: Syria says airspace partially closed
content: We have some lines from Syria’s Civil Aviation Authority announcing the closure of part of the country’s airspace. “The southern air corridors will be closed … from Saturday 12:00pm (09:00 GMT) for 12 hours,” the agency said in a statement.

title: Which military bases in the Middle East are hosting US troops?
content: The US and Israel have attacked multiple Iranian cities, prompting a fierce response from Tehran, which launched missiles at Israel and towards several military bases in the Middle East where US forces operate. The intense exchange of projectiles forced several nations to close their airspaces. Many countries had in recent days cautioned their diplomats and citizens in the region about the prospects of a military escalation between Iran and the US. Some countries had asked their citizens to evacuate. Iran had previously warned that if it were attacked, it would respond by targeting US military facilities across the region. Check out our comprehensive explainer to find out what these bases are.

title: At least one person killed in Abu Dhabi after UAE intercepts several Iranian missiles: Report
content: At least one person has been killed in Abu Dhabi, the capital of the United Arab Emirates, after several missiles launched from Iran were intercepted, according to the country’s state news agency. The UAE said “this attack is a flagrant violation of national sovereignty and international law,” and added that “it reserves its full right to respond to this escalation.”

title: Loud explosion heard in Tehran
content: A loud explosion has been heard in the Iranian capital, our colleagues are reporting. We’ll provide more information on this as we get it.

title: Qatar Airways suspending flights
content: Qatar Airways has suspended all flights due to the closure of Qatari airspace.

title: Iran’s Supreme National Security Council slams ‘brutal air operation’
content: The Supreme National Security Council of Iran has said the country was targeted by a “brutal air operation” carried out by the US and Israel. “This occurred once again during negotiations, and the enemy imagines that the resilient Iranian nation will surrender to their petty demands through these cowardly actions,” it said in a statement.

title: Turkish Airlines cancels flights to multiple destinations
content: Turkish Airlines has cancelled flights to Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, the UAE, Oman, Lebanon, Syria, Iraq, Iran and Jordan.

title: More explosions in Abu Dhabi and Dubai
content: We are getting multiple reports of explosions taking place in Dubai and Abu Dhabi. This is in addition to an earlier attack on Abu Dhabi where the airspace has been closed already.

title: Iran confirms it targeted sites in Gulf region
content: According to the Fars news agency, Iran has targeted: Al-Udeid Air Base in Qatar Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait Al-Dhafra Air Base in the UAE The US Fifth Fleet base in Bahrain

title: Explosions reported in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia
content: The AFP news agency is reporting explosions in Saudi Arabia’s Riyadh as well. We will bring you more as we have it.

title: Explosions heard in Urmia, western Iran
content: Explosions have been reported in Urmia, in the western part of Iran, shortly after the Israeli army said it was launching attacks on the area. An Israeli army spokesperson issued evacuation warnings for all residents near military installations in Iran.

title: Video shows explosion in Bahrain
content: We have verified footage of explosions in Juffair, Bahrain: Explosions hit Jufair, Bahrain, home to the US Fifth Fleet headquarters. pic.twitter.com/YjXTCeykOY — Josep Goded (@josepgoded) February 28, 2026

title: New explosion heard in Manama, Bahrain
content: The AFP news agency is reporting a new loud explosion after a US base in Bahrain’s capital came under a missile attack as Iran retaliates for US and Israeli strikes.

title: Multiple explosions heard in Qatar
content: Multiple loud explosions were heard in Doha, Qatar, shortly after the country’s Defense Ministry said all missiles launched from Iran were intercepted. We will keep you posted.

title: Qatar says missiles intercepted
content: Qatar’s Defense Ministry says “it has successfully thwarted a number of attacks targeting the country’s territory. “The Ministry of Defense confirms that the threat was dealt with immediately upon detection, in accordance with the pre-approved security plan, and that all missiles were intercepted before reaching Qatari territory.”

title: US military presence in the region
content: (Al Jazeera)

title: Explosions in Qatar
content: Explosions have now been heard in Qatar’s capital, Doha. This comes after Qatar’s Defense Ministry said missiles targeting the country were intercepted.

title: Israeli army conducting ‘broad strike’ on western Iran
content: The Israeli army has issued a statement saying it is “conducting a broad strike on a number of military targets belonging to the Iranian regime in western Iran”.

title: Recap of recent developments
content: Iran has targeted US forces in Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait and the United Arab Emirates. Bahrain has confirmed that the headquarters of the US Navy’s 5th Fleet has been targeted by a missile attack. Qatar’s Defence Ministry is saying an Iranian missile has been intercepted by the Patriot defence system. A loud explosion has been reported from the UAE capital, Abu Dhabi, the Reuters news agency has reported. Qatar, Kuwait and the UAE have closed their airspace. Earlier, Iran’s IRGC confirmed that it launched missiles and drones towards Israel following the US-Israeli attacks.

title: Iran says armed forces ‘fully prepared’ to make ‘aggressors regret their actions’
content: Iran’s Foreign Ministry says Israeli attacks this morning have targeted the country’s “territorial integrity and national sovereignty, including defensive infrastructure and non-military locations in various cities across the country”. “The renewed military aggression by the United States and the Zionist regime against Iran constitutes a violation of international law and the principles of the United Nations Charter. The Islamic Republic of Iran considers this aggression a clear breach of international peace and security and emphasises that it reserves its legitimate right to respond decisively,” the ministry said in a statement. “The Iranian nation has always demonstrated patience and restraint to prevent escalation and preserve regional stability,” it read, adding that the armed forces stand “fully prepared to defend the country and will make aggressors regret their actions”. The statement added that the attacks by the US and Israel were a “clear example of armed aggression” and cited Article 51 of the UN Charter about Iran’s right to self-defence. “History shows that Iranians have never surrendered to aggression. This time as well, Iran’s response will be decisive, and aggressors will regret their hostile actions,” it added.

title: Where have explosions been reported in the Middle East?
content: Explosions have been reported from: Abu Dhabi, UAE Manama, Bahrain Kuwait

title: Gulf countries close airspaces
content: Qatar, Kuwait and the UAE have closed their airspaces.

title: Russian official says US shows ‘true colours’ with attack on Iran
content: Dmitry Medvedev, a close ally of Russian President Vladimir Putin and currently the deputy chairman of Russia’s Security Council, has accused the US of using nuclear talks with Iran as a cover-up before military operations. “The peacemaker has once again shown his true colours,” Medvedev said on Telegram. “All negotiations with Iran are a cover operation. No one doubted that. No one really wanted to agree on anything.”

title: Explosion reported in Abu Dhabi
content: A loud explosion has been reported from the UAE capital, Abu Dhabi, Reuters news agency has reported.

title: Explosions heard in Kuwait, sirens activated
content: Our colleagues at Al Jazeera Arabic have reported explosions were heard in Kuwait. Sirens have been activated, the report said.

title: Bahrain says headquarters of the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet targeted
content: Bahrain has confirmed that the headquarters of the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet has been targeted by a missile attack. [Screengrab/Al Jazeera]

title: Netanyahu ‘changing the agenda’ to declare victory
content: Akiva Elder, an Israeli political analyst, says Netanyahu is promising regime change, and he is the “angel to save the Iranian people from their evil regime”. “I am quite sure that the Israelis, and this is about 50 percent of the Jewish population, don’t take him seriously. He wasn’t able to declare victory after the massacre in Gaza and the thousands of victims in Israel,” Elder told Al Jazeera from Tel Aviv. “His total victory is changing the agenda.” Elder added that Netanyahu is “doing what he does best” and brainwashing people that there is “no option other than perpetuate the war against our neighbours”.

title: Smoke seen in Bahrain after reports of explosions
content: The Reuters news agency is reporting that smoke is seen from Bahrain’s Juffair area housing a US Navy Base.

title: Qatar advises all residents to stay indoors, away from military sites
content: Qatar has issued an alert on mobile devices, recommending all residents to stay indoors, whether at home or elsewhere, and away from military bases.

title: Explosions in Bahrain
content: We are getting reports that there have been explosions taking place in Bahrain. Earlier, Bahrain’s Interior Ministry issued an emergency alert and urged citizens to go to the nearest safe location.

title: Iran repeatedly denied pursuing nuclear weapons before attacks
content: Iran has long denied wanting to create a nuclear bomb. Iran’s President Pezeshkian on Thursday denied Trump’s accusation that Tehran was pursuing the bomb and said Iran would not have nuclear weapons. Earlier this week, Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi told the UN Conference on Disarmament that Iran would “never seek nuclear weapons”, calling for complete disarmament and for peaceful use of nuclear energy.

title: Iranian missile intercepted over Qatar
content: Qatar’s Defence Ministry is saying that an Iranian missile has been intercepted by the Patriot defence system.

title: All US, Israel assets in region a ‘legitimate target’, says senior Iranian official
content: A senior Iranian official has told Al Jazeera that they are telling Israel to “prepare for what is coming, and our response will be public, and there are no red lines”. “All American and Israeli assets and interests in the Middle East have become a legitimate target. There are no red lines after this aggression, and everything is possible, including scenarios that were not previously considered,” the official said. “The United States and Israel have started an aggression and a war that will have wide and long-lasting repercussions. We were not surprised by the joint American-Israeli aggression and we have a complex response with no time limit,” the official stated, adding that any calls for Iran to show restraint or surrender are “unacceptable and mere wishful thinking”.

title: Various international flights suspended, airspaces closed
content: Wizz Air has suspended all flights to and from Israel, the UAE’s Dubai, as well as Abu Dhabi and Jordan’s Amman with immediate effect until March 7 Lufthansa suspended Saturday and Sunday flights to and from Dubai, Tel Aviv, Lebanon’s Beirut and Oman’s Muscat KLM cancels flights from Amsterdam to Tel Aviv Oman Air suspends all flights to Iraq’s capital, Baghdad Russia suspends all flights to Iran and Israel Kuwait suspends all flights to Iran until further notice Iraq cancels all inbound and outbound flights, closing its airspace Israel cancels all civilian flights, closing its airspace

title: Photos: People take shelter as sirens are heard in Tel Aviv
content: [Ohad Zwigenberg/AP Photo] [Jack Guez/AFP] [ilia Yefimovich/AFP] [ilia Yefimovich/AFP]

title: Iran’s Interior Ministry urges citizens to remain calm
content: Iran’s Interior Ministry says the “criminal enemy, once again, in violation of all international laws and during negotiations, has attacked our dear homeland again”, Tasnim news agency reported. In a statement, the ministry issued an order to governors across the country to remind them of previous instructions and the “prompt” reports on the situation in the provinces and “the mobilisation of all facilities to address the urgent needs of the people”.

title: Iran says it has launched missiles and drones towards Israel
content: Iran’s IRGC has confirmed that it has launched missiles and drones towards Israel following the US-Israeli attacks. “In response to the aggression of the hostile and criminal enemy against the Islamic Republic of Iran, the first wave of extensive missile and drone attacks by the Islamic Republic of Iran towards the occupied territories has begun.”

title: Iran in midst of near-total internet blackout: NetBlocks
content: We have an update from NetBlocks, the global internet watchdog, about connectivity currently in Iran. In a social media post, it said network data show Iran is now “in the midst of a near-total internet blackout”, with national connectivity at 4 percent. “The incident comes amid US and Israeli combat operations and matches measures used during last year’s war with Israel,” it added. ⚠️ Confirmed: Network data show #Iran is now in the midst of a near-total internet blackout with national connectivity at 4% or ordinary levels. The incident comes amid US and Israeli combat operations and matches measures used during last year's war with Israel. pic.twitter.com/1XunOr4Q19 — NetBlocks (@netblocks) February 28, 2026

title: WATCH: Has Iran rigged the game against itself?
content: Iran has a long history of coups, revolutions and invasions, leaving it with a complex web of economic, religious and military alliances. On paper, it’s meant to create a balance between theocracy and democracy. In reality, only one man is truly in control.

title: Mapped out: Attacks on Iran
content: (Al Jazeera)

title: New explosions heard in Iran
content: Iran’s Tasnim news agency has reported that “two or more” explosions had been heard in Tehran. The IRNA news agency also reported explosions were heard in the western and southern areas of Ilam city. Moreover, air defence confrontations with “hostile targets” were continuing in the city centre and other parts of Tehran, Tasnim added.

title: Qatar says security situation ‘stable’ amid Israeli attack on Iran
content: Qatar’s Interior Ministry has confirmed that the situation in the country is “stable and secure” following Israel’s attack on Iran. In a statement, the ministry said security agencies were monitoring the situation in a way that “ensures the preservation of public security and the safety of citizens, residents and visitors, and the continuity of services without interruption”.

title: US envoy to Israel thanks Trump
content: Mike Huckabee, the US ambassador to Israel and an avowed Christian Zionist who last week prompted outrage when he suggested it would be “fine” if Israel expanded across the Middle East, has issued a statement on social media thanking Trump for launching the attack against Iran. Thank you @POTUS for strong American leadership to stop the killing and threats to Americans for the past 47 years. You gave them many chances to do it peacefully & they said no. Even as I sit in the bullseye of it, I am grateful for a strong President & a strong America. https://t.co/COXMrFaDTB — Ambassador Mike Huckabee (@GovMikeHuckabee) February 28, 2026

title: Netanyahu says attack aims to remove ‘existential threat’
content: Israel’s PM Netanyahu has said the attacks on Iran aimed to remove an “existential threat”. Netanyahu added that “joint action” by Israel and the US “will create the conditions for the brave Iranian people to take their fate into their own hands” and praised Trump for his “historic leadership”.

title: ‘Additional barrage of missiles launched towards Israel,’ army says
content: The Israeli army is saying that “an additional barrage of missiles was launched toward Israel”.

title: ‘Too early’ to know success of Israeli attack
content: Al Jazeera’s Ali Hashem, who has covered Iran extensively, explained that given that this is a “deja vu moment”, referencing the 12-day war last June, it would be “unlikely” that high Iranian officials would be in their expected areas. “For now, what we know is the President Pezeskian isn’t harmed, the supreme leader wasn’t in the complex. But, this attack, it’s clear, is mainly aimed at decapitating [the political elite],” Hashem explained. “How successful or unsuccessful this was, that is too early to tell.”

title: Internet disruption across Iran
content: Netblocks is reporting disruption to internet access in parts of Iran, including the capital Tehran, “with connectivity at 54% of ordinary levels”. ⚠️ Confirmed: Live metrics show a disruption to internet access in parts of #Iran including Tehran with connectivity at 54% of ordinary levels. The incident comes as the US and Israel target the regime with military strikes and will limit visibility into incidents on the ground. pic.twitter.com/YvKpsa8d6x — NetBlocks (@netblocks) February 28, 2026

title: What we know so far
content: Missiles have struck several areas in Tehran, where ministries and military compounds are located. Explosions have also been reported in Iranian cities including Kermanshah, Lorestan, Tabriz, Isfahan and Karaj. President Trump said US has begun “major combat operation” in Iran aimed at “eliminating threats from the Iranian regime”. The Israeli army said missiles were launched from Iran, prompting sirens in several areas of the country. Israel has declared a state of emergency and advised its citizens to stay close to shelters. A US official has told Al Jazeera that joint US-Israeli air strikes aim to dismantle Iran’s security apparatus.

title: Explosions rock northern Israel
content: Explosions have rocked northern Israel as the country worked to intercept incoming Iranian missiles after launching a nationwide attack with the US on Iran. The blasts echoed just after the Israeli military said it would be using its air defence systems to bring down the Iranian fire. There was no immediate word on any damage or casualties from the ongoing attack.

title: British embassy in Qatar issues advisory for its nationals
content: The British embassy in Qatar has recommended its nationals in the Gulf country shelter in place until further notice “out of an abundance of caution”. “We are monitoring the situation” amid “heightened risk of regional tension” that could lead to “travel disruption and other unanticipated impacts”, the advisory said on X. Stay updated on Travel Advice by signing up for the alerts on https://t.co/rIYNetOb4I pic.twitter.com/P8Tilm5xCy — UK in Qatar 🇬🇧🇶🇦 (@ukinqatar) February 28, 2026

title: Photos: Airlines avoiding Iranian airspace following attacks
content: [Screengrab/Flightradar24]

title: US setting table for Iranian ‘revolution’
content: The United States has once again set the table for a revolution in Iran. They’ve done it before. This time, they’re doing it with weapons and bombs rather than covertly through the CIA. It’s clear that this is going to be a continuous military operation, with Donald Trump accepting the fact that there may be casualties. He said it’s something that you have to accept in war, and urged the Iranian people to sit tight for the moment, but then seize control of their government.

title: US Embassy in Qatar issues ‘shelter-in-place’ advisory
content: Message for U.S. Citizens: The U.S. Embassy in Qatar is implementing a shelter-in-place for all personnel. We recommend all Americans do the same until further notice. Actions to Take: – Find a secure location within your residence or another safe building. Have a supply of… pic.twitter.com/ENogjpfK5r — U.S. Embassy in Qatar (@USEmbassyDoha) February 28, 2026

title: Iran preparing to ‘take revenge’ on Israel: State TV
content: Iran is preparing to “take revenge” on Israel and deliver “strong response”, State TV reported. As we reported, the Israeli army says missiles have been launched towards Israel from Iran.

title: Iran launches missiles towards Israel: Israeli army
content: The Israeli army says missiles launched from Iran were identified heading towards Israel, prompting sirens in several areas of the country. “The public is requested to follow the instructions of the Home Front Command,” it said. “At this time, the Israeli Air Force is operating to intercept and strike threats where necessary to remove the threat. “The defence is not hermetic, and therefore it is essential that the public continue to adhere to the Home Front Command’s guidelines. The public is requested to continue to follow the instructions of the Home Front Command.”

title: Photos: US and Israel strike Tehran
content: [AP Photo] [Getty Images] [Atta Kenare/AFP]

title: Poland’s Tusk says embassy staff safe, prepared for various scenarios
content: We have some comments from Poland’s Prime Minister Donald Tusk, who had urged his compatriots to leave Iran immediately on February 19. “At this moment, our citizens, including the staff of the Polish ⁠embassy in Tehran, ⁠are safe, but we are prepared ⁠for various scenarios,” Tusk ⁠wrote on ⁠X. “The unity of Poles on the issue of ‌security is particularly important today.” Check out which countries had urged their citizens to leave Iran in this story .

title: US military to carry out multiday operation against Iran: Report
content: The US is planning a multiday operation against Iran, a US official has told Reuters news agency.

title: WATCH: Explosions heard in Tehran as Israel launches attacks
US embassy in Bahrain implements shelter-in-place for all personnel
content: The US embassy in Bahrain has implemented a shelter-in-place for all personnel and recommend all US citizens do the same until further notice. “Find a secure location within your residence or another safe building. Have a supply of food, water, medications, and other essential items,” the alert said on X. There was a similar measure announced earlier by the US embassy in Qatar.

title: US, Israel strike Iran amid talks yet again
content: Talks over Iran’s nuclear programme have again been abruptly cut off by US-Israeli strikes on Iran. In June last year, US attacks targeted Iran during talks. The US’s Operation Midnight Hammer came towards the end of a 12-day war Israel initiated with Iran that month. Since those strikes, Trump has repeatedly hailed the attack, saying it eliminated Iran’s nuclear programme and led to “peace” in the Middle East. Eight months later, the US and Israel have launched yet another attack as both sides had just ended a round of talks in Geneva.

title: Concern in Lebanon at being ‘dragged’ into Israel, US, Iran conflict
content: In the past few days there has been an escalation, a pattern of escalation. On Thursday night, for example, the Israeli army carried out a wave of air strikes in the east of the country, the Bekaa area, an area which the Israeli military believes is the operational base of Hezbollah. So this escalation of attacks has really caused a lot of concern in Lebanon that the country could be dragged into a potential conflict, which is now happening, between Israel, Iran and the United States, because Hezbollah is Iran’s strategic and ideological ally.

title: No official statement from Iranian gov’t over attack
content: Until now, we haven’t had any official statement from the Iranian government but we have lots of lines from the Fars news agency. It has been talking about strikes not only in Tehran but also in several other cities, including Qom, not far from the capital, and also Isfahan, at the centre of the country. News lines talk about strikes on one of the most important airports in Iran and also the district of Pasteur, where the presidential palace is, but so far, we do not have any confirmation, any information about any high-ranking officials being killed in the attack. There are reports of people being wounded in the area of Pasteur, and they are being evacuated to hospitals.

title: More from Trump
content: We are going to destroy their missiles and raze their missile industry to the ground. We are going to annihilate their navy. We are going to ensure that the region’s ‘terrorist’ proxies can no longer destabilise the region or the world. We will ensure that Iran does not obtain a nuclear weapon. It is a very simple message. They will never have a nuclear weapon. This regime will soon learn that no one should challenge the strength and might of the United States Armed Forces.

title: What areas have Israeli attacks targeted in Tehran?
content: So far, Israeli attacks on the Iranian capital have targeted: The Ministry of Intelligence The Ministry of Defence Atomic Energy Organization of Iran Parchin military complex

title: Iranian news agencies, including IRNA, facing cyberattacks
content: Iran’s Tasnim news agency has reported that Iranian news websites, including the Iranian state news agency IRNA, are facing cyberattacks. So far, IRNA’s Telegram account is still functioning but its website remains inaccessible.

title: Israel intensifies attacks on Lebanon as it targets Iran
content: In the past hour, Israel has carried out a series of air strikes targeting a number of areas, particularly in the Iqlim al-Tuffah region of Lebanon. What we have seen in recent days is an Israeli escalation in attacks focusing on what it says it believes to be strategic infrastructure belonging to Hezbollah. The Israeli army believes the group has rocket launchers and long-range missiles. The question that has been repeatedly asked in Lebanon in the past days is what Hezbollah will do in the event of an Israeli attack on Iran. But the question is also what Israel will do and whether it will carry out preemptive attacks on Hezbollah to eliminate all possible threats coming from this front.

title: President Pezeshkian remains unharmed: State media
content: Iran’s IRNA news agency, quoting a source in the presidential office, is reporting that President Pezeshkian remains unharmed.

title: Sources suggest US aiming to ‘decapitate’ Iran’s government
content: I’ve been messaging my sources who suggest that the US involvement in this attack is aimed at “decapitating the Iranian regime”. They say that from what they can understand, the attacks were concentrated on areas where Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei may be sheltering, so the intention was to try and take out the head of the regime and then see what would happen afterwards. Some countries have been cautioning the US against this attack and wanted to know what the plan for the day after would be, simply because you can’t guarantee that by removing the supreme leader, you would necessarily form a pro-US government.

title: Many in Trump’s MAGA movement see Iran attack as ‘stupid foreign war’
content: There is no guarantee that the United States would walk away from this – like they did with the strikes in June – essentially unscathed. There could be retaliation and as soon as American troops, American service personnel are injured or killed, that would turn a great deal of pressure against Donald Trump. Not least because he was the man who said he was elected to stop what he described as “stupid foreign wars”. Many in his own MAGA movement see a war against Iran – half a world away – as a stupid foreign war.

title: Trump claims Iran attacks aim ‘to defend the American people’
content: US President Donald Trump says the joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran aim at “eliminating imminent threats from the Iranian regime”. “Short time ago, US military began major combat operation in Iran. Our objective is to defend the American people by eliminating threats from the Iranian regime,” he said.

title: Hospitals on alert in Iran, ambulances dispatched
content: Iran’s Health Ministry spokesperson says that hospitals are on alert, Tasnim news outlet reported.

title: ‘We warned you,’ says Iran’s national security commission head
content: We have some comments from Ebrahim Azizi, the head of the national security commission of the Iranian parliament. “We warned you!” he wrote on social media. “Now you have started down a path which end is no longer in your control,” he added. We warned you! Now you have started down a path which end is no longer in your control. — ابراهیم عزیزی (@Ebrahimazizi33) February 28, 2026

title: Iran to respond with crushing attacks, Iranian official says
content: An Iranian official has told the Reuters news agency that Tehran is preparing for a response to US-Israeli attacks, and the counterattacks are set to be crushing.

title: Attack on Iran could suggest ‘one and done’ strike to secure deal
content: I was speaking to someone in the last few hours while speculation was growing of the possibility of some sort of US military action, and he suggested that if there was military action, it was likely to take the form, in his words, “one and done”. That there would be one strike, it would be in his view, to focus Iranian attention and say, “Look, this is what we’re doing just now, but there could be a lot worse ahead if you don’t come to some sort of deal.” We heard from the Omani negotiator in Geneva, who felt there was movement towards the deal, that it might take a bit of time. But the heart of the deal was there. That simply wasn’t reflected in what Donald Trump was saying as he flew to Texas on Friday; he said that he was upset with the Iranians, and he hoped there would be a deal.

title: Iran preparing for retaliation, official says: Report
content: ⁠Iran ⁠is preparing ⁠for retaliation following the attacks, with the ⁠response set ⁠to be ⁠crushing, an ⁠Iranian ⁠official has told Reuters.

title: Explosions heard in various cities across Iran
content: Explosions have also been reported in the cities of Kermanshah, Lorestan, Tabriz, Isfahan and Karaj, according to local media. Al Jazeera could not immediately verify the report.

title: Iraq closes airspace following attacks on Iran
content: Iraq’s Transport Ministry ⁠says it has closed its national airspace ‌following the attacks on Iran, according to the Iraqi News Agency INA.

title: Speculation likely that Israel attacked to ‘force hand’ of US to take action
content: People were expecting that there may well be an attack by the United States. Donald Trump had been talking about it. Most people expected some sort of United States action. So there is surprise that Israel has gone first. But there will be speculation here in the United States – inevitably – that this is Benjamin Netanyahu once again trying to force the United States to take action against Iran. And the reason he has done this is to try and force their hand.

title: US-Israel strikes aim to dismantle Iran’s security apparatus: US official
content: A US official has told Al Jazeera that joint US-Israeli air strikes aim to dismantle Iran’s security apparatus.

title: Iran shuts down airspace until further notice
content: Majid Akhavan, spokesperson for Iran’s Civil Aviation Organization, has announced in an interview with the Mehr News Agency that “the entire country’s airspace has been closed until further notice”.

title: Iran’s mobile network disrupted
content: Cellphone communications have been disrupted across several areas of Iran’s capital, Tehran. No calls are possible at present. There’s a chance the internet might be disrupted soon, as well.

title: Israel forced Trump’s hand against Iran
content: Mehran Kamrava, director of the Iranian studies unit at the Arab Centre for Research and Policy Studies and professor at Georgetown University in Qatar, says that while he did not think that the US would attack Iran, Israel has been “quite vocal about its intentions to sabotage US-Iran negotiations”. “As it did last June, it appears to have launched an attack designed to derail the negotiations between Iran and the US,” Kamrava told Al Jazeera. “I think [US President] Donald Trump had pushed himself into a corner from which he couldn’t back out. The so-called armada, the massive military build – up, the constant green lights to Israel over the last year and a half, and so I think in many ways, yet again, what we see is the Israelis forced Donald Trump’s hand and he couldn’t really say no to them at this juncture,” Kamrava explained.

title: Photos: Smoke rises over Tehran
content: A plume of smoke rises following an explosion in Tehran [AFP] [AFP] [Atta Kenare/AFP]

title: Israel declares state of emergency amid Iran strikes
content: The Israeli army has declared a state of emergency and asked citizens to stay close to shelters. We understand that this is a campaign that Israel has launched in line with its stated policy that Iran poses a threat to Israel’s safety. At the moment, details are scant but a strike on Iran would certainly not be happening without US approval.

author_tagline: Reporting from Ramallah in the occupied West Bank
title: Israel, US assessed ‘no path for negotiations’ despite diplomatic efforts
content: Several rounds of negotiations, of course, have taken place [to avoid a conflict], the last of which was just two days ago. Then, the Omani foreign minister, who was the chief negotiator in those indirect talks between the US and Iran, went to Washington and spoke with the US Vice President JD Vance, just in the past 12 hours, hoping that his efforts could avert a confrontation. But now we know that the confrontation started from the Israeli front. Let me just update our viewers that Israeli airspace has been closed. Israeli security sources are now saying that Israel and the United States have assessed in the past 24 hours that there was no path for negotiations.

title: US launches joint strikes with Israel on Iran, US official tells Al Jazeera
content: A US official has told Al Jazeera that the attacks on Iran were carried out as a joint military operation by the US and Israel.

title: US embassy in Qatar implements shelter-in-place for all personnel after Israeli strikes on Iran
content: ⁠The ⁠US embassy in Qatar ⁠implemented shelter-in-place for ⁠all personnel, recommending that all of ‌its citizens do the same until further notice. The ⁠move comes ⁠after Israel launched an attack against ⁠Iran ⁠on Saturday.

title: Israel sounds sirens across country for residents to stay close to protected areas
content: The Israeli military has announced that sirens had sounded throughout the country for residents to stay close to protected spaces. In a statement on Telegram, the army said the alerts are to prepare the public for the possibility of missiles being launched towards Israel. It added in a separate statement that all areas of the country would be shifted from “full activity” to “essential activity”, including the closure of schools, a ban on gatherings and the closure of workplaces, except for essential sectors.

title: Two bombs heard in western Tehran
content: Several missiles have struck University Street and the Jomhouri area in Tehran, the Fars news agency reports. Our correspondent, who is in western Tehran, heard two bombs explode.

title: Thanks for joining us
content: Hello and welcome to our coverage of the developments in Iran and the region as Israel strikes Tehran. We will keep you updated.
